name: Fuente Álamo - Corvera - Saladares del Guadalentín Loop
name_original: Puerto del Saladillo - Corvera - La Loma — vuelta desde Fuente Álamo de Murcia
description: This moderate 32.0-mile (51.5 km) road cycling route near Fuente Álamo de Murcia offers well-paved surfaces and rural scenery.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Fuente Álamo De Murcia traverse a diverse landscape characterized by extensive open plains and significant mountainous elevations. The region features well-paved surfaces through rural scenery, agricultural fields, and more challenging sections in ranges like Sierra de Carrascoy. This varied topography provides options for different cycling levels, from gentle rides to routes with substantial elevation changes. Best road cycling routes around Fuente Álamo De Murcia The most popular road cycling route is Fuente Álamo - Corvera - Saladares del Guadalentín Loop, a 32.0 miles (51.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route features rolling terrain with moderate ascents, suitable for various fitness levels. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto del Garruchal Road – Garruchal Pass loop from Fuente Álamo de Murcia, a moderate 60.1 miles (96.7 km) path. This route includes notable elevation gains, winding through the region's hilly sections and offering varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Bolnuevo Beach – Castellar Beach loop from Fuente Álamo de Murcia, a 37.1 miles (59.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas and varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Fuente Álamo De Murcia is defined by open plains, mountainous elevations, and agricultural fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult climbs. The routes in Fuente Álamo De Murcia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Fuente Álamo De Murcia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Fuente Álamo de Murcia?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes around Fuente Álamo de Murcia, offering a diverse range of options for cyclists. The komoot community has explored these routes over 600 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Fuente Álamo de Murcia?
A: Road cycling routes in Fuente Álamo de Murcia feature a diverse landscape. You'll find extensive open plains, picturesque agricultural fields, and significant mountainous elevations in ranges like Sierra de Carrascoy. This blend provides varied experiences, from gentle rides to challenging climbs.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Fuente Álamo de Murcia?
A: Yes, Fuente Álamo de Murcia offers several routes suitable for beginners. There are 9 easy routes available, perfect for those looking for less strenuous rides. These routes often traverse rural scenery with moderate ascents.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in Fuente Álamo de Murcia?
A: For more challenging rides, look towards the mountainous elevations. Ranges like Sierra de Carrascoy, Algarrobo, and Pinilla offer routes with significant elevation gains. An example of a difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38268385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road to Mazarrón – Pinar Viewpoint loop from Fuente Álamo de Murcia&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 124.5 km with over 1,700 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, the region caters to long-distance cyclists. You can find routes extending over 100 km, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38268385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road to Mazarrón – Pinar Viewpoint loop from Fuente Álamo de Murcia&lt;/a&gt;, which is 124.5 km long. These routes often include substantial elevation changes, providing a comprehensive cycling experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Fuente Álamo de Murcia?
A: Fuente Álamo de Murcia benefits from a pleasant year-round climate, making it suitable for road cycling in most seasons. The mild winters and sunny conditions are particularly appealing, though summer rides might require early starts to avoid the midday heat.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I discover along road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning vistas. You can cycle towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6581220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Blancas Summit&lt;/a&gt; for impressive views combining white cliffs with the distant sea, or climb to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2857853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Águila&lt;/a&gt; in Sierra de Carrascoy for panoramic views of the Murcia region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3067287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cedacero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offers great scenery.

Q: Are there any natural features or landmarks to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the routes, the region is rich in natural beauty. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1462793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Cañar&lt;/a&gt;, a natural monument, or explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3950819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minas de Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra de Carrascoy National Park, with its pine forests, is also a prominent natural feature.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Fuente Álamo de Murcia?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes both flat plains and challenging mountain sections, as well as the well-paved surfaces and scenic rural backdrops.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Fuente Álamo de Murcia?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Fuente Álamo de Murcia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010650011/fuente-alamo-corvera-saladares-del-guadalentin-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Álamo - Corvera - Saladares del Guadalentín Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1673652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Garruchal Road – Garruchal Pass loop from Fuente Álamo de Murcia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find coastal road cycling routes near Fuente Álamo de Murcia?
A: Yes, Fuente Álamo de Murcia's proximity to the Costa Cálida means you can access coastal routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38261844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolnuevo Beach – Castellar Beach loop from Fuente Álamo de Murcia&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, leading through coastal areas and varied terrain.

Q: Are there routes that combine rural scenery with more challenging sections?
A: Absolutely. The region's diverse topography ensures routes that blend extensive agricultural fields and rural scenery with hillier sections. You can enjoy picturesque rides through fruit and vegetable cultivations before encountering more demanding climbs in the surrounding mountain ranges.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebikes and typically takes around 2 hours and 12 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate for continuous cycling and may vary based on your pace and any stops you make.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features mostly well-paved surfaces, ensuring a smooth ride. You'll experience rolling inland terrain, passing through agricultural areas with citrus orchards, almond groves, and livestock farms.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this route as 'moderate' and recommends 'good fitness'. While the elevation gain of approximately 280 meters over 51.5 kilometers is manageable, it's not ideal for absolute beginners. Cyclists with some experience and a reasonable fitness level will find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque views typical of the Murcia region's agricultural landscape, including citrus orchards, almond groves, and livestock farms. The 'Puerto del Saladillo' in the route's name suggests you'll encounter elevated areas offering scenic vistas. The area around Fuente Álamo de Murcia also offers rural charm.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several points of interest. You'll go through the settlement of Pueblo del Brillante and Valladolises y Lo Jurado. You'll also ride along the RM-601 road in Corvera, which is a recognized cycle way.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Murcia region is a significant destination for cycling, and generally, the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months offer the most pleasant weather for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer. These seasons provide comfortable temperatures and often clear skies.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact starting point aren't detailed, Fuente Álamo de Murcia is an ideal base for outdoor activities. You should be able to find suitable parking within or near Fuente Álamo de Murcia or Corvera, which are key points on the route, to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with the Vía Verde del Campo de Cartagena at various points, offering potential connections to other cycling trails in the region.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route. However, please note that parts of the area are within the Zona de Especial Protección para Aves Saladares del Guadalentín, a special protection area for birds. Always respect local regulations and wildlife when cycling through protected zones.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, primarily on paved surfaces. While dogs are generally allowed in public areas, bringing a dog on a racebike route of this distance and nature is not typically recommended for their safety or comfort. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured and consider the length and speed of the ride.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through settlements like Corvera and Fuente Álamo de Murcia. These towns are likely to have cafes, pubs, or small shops where you can stop for refreshments, food, or to refill water bottles.
